page_content:
ARAB322 Arabic for Daily Life II (3 Credits)

Develops students’ speaking and listening abilities in both formal Arabic and a dialect, aiming to bring them to the advanced low level in speaking. Focus on activating vocabulary and grammar structures, speaking at

the paragraph level, self-correction, and using both formal and informal registers of Arabic.

Prerequisite: ARAB202; or equivalent, or must have appropriate World Language Placement (WLP) score.

ARAB325 Structure and Function of the Arabic Language (3 Credits) Facilitates logic-based understanding of Arabic grammar and syntax. Explores different grammatical structures and related elements, including through writing and translation.

Prerequisite: ARAB102; or must have appropriate World Language Placement (WLP) score.

Credit Only Granted for. ARAB398I! or ARAB325.
